pairs.match: Standard alias for pyplot, provides MATLAB-like interface
wrong_matches: Creates a line plot from x and y
Displays the current figure
pairs.feedback_correct: Correct! This is the conventional import statement for matplotlib's pyplot.
pairs.feedback_wrong: Incorrect. That describes a plotting function or display command, not the import convention.

pairs.match: Line plot connecting consecutive data points
wrong_matches: Scatter plot with markers only
Histogram with bins
pairs.feedback_correct: Correct! plt.plot() draws lines between points to show trends.
pairs.feedback_wrong: Incorrect. This is not the function for scatter plots or histograms.

pairs.match: Scatter plot with markers, no connecting lines
wrong_matches: Line plot connecting points
Set y-axis tick labels
pairs.feedback_correct: Correct! plt.scatter() shows individual markers, useful for distributions.
pairs.feedback_wrong: Incorrect. That describes a line plot or tick customization.

pairs.match: Displays the current figure; must be called after plotting
wrong_matches: Adds a title to the plot
Sets the x-axis label
pairs.feedback_correct: Correct! Without plt.show(), the plot is created but not visible.
pairs.feedback_wrong: Incorrect. That describes labeling or titling functions.

pairs.match: Divides data into bins and counts frequencies per bin
wrong_matches: Plots individual points without lines
Connects points to show trends over time
pairs.feedback_correct: Correct! Histograms show the distribution of a dataset.
pairs.feedback_wrong: Incorrect. That describes scatter plots or line plots.

pairs.match: Creates a histogram with 3 equal-width bins
wrong_matches: Creates a line plot with 3 data points
Creates a scatter plot with 3 markers
pairs.feedback_correct: Correct! The bins parameter controls granularity.
pairs.feedback_wrong: Incorrect. plt.hist is for histograms, not line or scatter plots.

pairs.match: Adds a label to the x-axis
wrong_matches: Adds a label to the y-axis
Adds a title above the plot
pairs.feedback_correct: Correct! xlabel labels the horizontal axis.
pairs.feedback_wrong: Incorrect. That would be ylabel or title.

pairs.match: Adds a label to the y-axis
wrong_matches: Adds a label to the x-axis
Sets custom tick positions on y-axis
pairs.feedback_correct: Correct! ylabel labels the vertical axis.
pairs.feedback_wrong: Incorrect. That describes xlabel or yticks.

pairs.match: Adds a title above the plot
wrong_matches: Adds a label to the x-axis
Displays the figure window
pairs.feedback_correct: Correct! title provides a descriptive heading.
pairs.feedback_wrong: Incorrect. That describes xlabel or show.

pairs.match: Customizes y-axis tick positions and labels
wrong_matches: Creates a line plot with population data
Displays the current figure
pairs.feedback_correct: Correct! yticks sets tick locations and optional custom labels.
pairs.feedback_wrong: Incorrect. That describes a plotting function or display command.

pairs.match: Unordered collection of key-value pairs with unique immutable keys
wrong_matches: 2D labeled data structure with columns of different types
1D labeled array, like a single column
pairs.feedback_correct: Correct! Dictionaries map keys to values.
pairs.feedback_wrong: Incorrect. That describes a DataFrame or Series.

pairs.match: Key-value pair in a dictionary
wrong_matches: Accessing a value using square brackets
Adding a new key with assignment
pairs.feedback_correct: Correct! A key-value pair is key: value inside curly braces.
pairs.feedback_wrong: Incorrect. That describes dictionary access or addition.

pairs.match: Returns the value associated with key 'albania'
wrong_matches: Adds a new key 'albania' with value
Deletes the key 'albania'
pairs.feedback_correct: Correct! Square brackets retrieve the value for a given key.
pairs.feedback_wrong: Incorrect. That would be assignment or del.

pairs.term: Why can't a list be a dict key?
pairs.match: Lists are mutable (unhashable); keys must be immutable
wrong_matches: Lists can be keys if they contain only numbers
Lists are not allowed because they are too large
pairs.feedback_correct: Correct! Immutability ensures the hash value never changes.
pairs.feedback_wrong: Incorrect. Mutable objects cannot be hashed, hence cannot be keys.

pairs.match: 2D labeled structure with columns of potentially different types
wrong_matches: 1D labeled array similar to a single column
Unordered key-value pairs
pairs.feedback_correct: Correct! DataFrame is the core pandas data structure like a spreadsheet.
pairs.feedback_wrong: Incorrect. That describes a Series or dictionary.

pairs.match: Reads CSV into DataFrame, using first column as row index
wrong_matches: Exports DataFrame to CSV file
Filters rows based on condition
pairs.feedback_correct: Correct! read_csv loads tabular data; index_col sets row labels.
pairs.feedback_wrong: Incorrect. That would be to_csv or DataFrame filtering.

pairs.match: Returns a pandas Series (1D labeled array)
wrong_matches: Returns a pandas DataFrame with one column
Returns a Python list of country names
pairs.feedback_correct: Correct! Single bracket on a DataFrame returns a Series.
pairs.feedback_wrong: Incorrect. That describes double brackets (DataFrame) or a list conversion.

pairs.match: <, <=, >, >=, ==, != return booleans
wrong_matches: and, or, not combine booleans
= assigns a value
pairs.feedback_correct: Correct! These compare values and yield True/False.
pairs.feedback_wrong: Incorrect. Those are logical operators or assignment.

pairs.match: Logical operators that combine boolean values
wrong_matches: Element-wise logical operations on arrays
Comparison operators like == and !=
pairs.feedback_correct: Correct! and, or, not work on single booleans.
pairs.feedback_wrong: Incorrect. That describes np.logical_ functions or comparisons.

pairs.match: Element-wise AND on arrays; returns boolean array
wrong_matches: Element-wise OR on arrays
Python 'and' operator (raises ValueError on arrays)
pairs.feedback_correct: Correct! This is the array-safe version of and.
pairs.feedback_wrong: Incorrect. That would be logical_or or the unsafe Python and.

pairs.match: Element-wise OR on arrays; returns True if any input True per element
wrong_matches: Element-wise NOT (negation) on arrays
Python 'or' operator (fails on arrays)
pairs.feedback_correct: Correct! logical_or works element-wise on array-like inputs.
pairs.feedback_wrong: Incorrect. That describes logical_not or unsafe Python or.

pairs.match: Element-wise NOT: flips True to False, False to True
wrong_matches: Python 'not' operator (fails on arrays)
Element-wise AND on arrays
pairs.feedback_correct: Correct! logical_not negates each element.
pairs.feedback_wrong: Incorrect. That describes logical_and or unsafe not.

pairs.match: Conditional execution: runs block if condition True
wrong_matches: Repeated execution while condition True
Iterates over each item in a sequence
pairs.feedback_correct: Correct! if runs the indented block only when condition is True.
pairs.feedback_wrong: Incorrect. That describes while or for loops.

pairs.match: Executes if preceding if condition is False
wrong_matches: Checks another condition if previous was False
Defines a block that always runs regardless
pairs.feedback_correct: Correct! else provides an alternative path.
pairs.feedback_wrong: Incorrect. That describes elif or a try-finally block.

pairs.match: Short for 'else if'; checks condition only if previous if/elif were False
wrong_matches: Executes regardless of conditions
Runs multiple blocks if conditions are True
pairs.feedback_correct: Correct! elif chains test conditions sequentially.
pairs.feedback_wrong: Incorrect. That would be unconditional code or multiple ifs.

pairs.match: Repeats block as long as condition is True; must update condition
wrong_matches: Iterates fixed number of times over a sequence
Conditional execution once
pairs.feedback_correct: Correct! while loops need an update to avoid infinite loops.
pairs.feedback_wrong: Incorrect. That describes for loops or if statements.

pairs.match: Iterates over each item in a sequence (list, string, etc.)
wrong_matches: Repeats while condition True
Executes block once if condition True
pairs.feedback_correct: Correct! for loops iterate over iterables.
pairs.feedback_wrong: Incorrect. That describes while or if.

pairs.match: Returns index-value pairs when iterating
wrong_matches: Iterates over dictionary items
Loops over array elements in row-major order
pairs.feedback_correct: Correct! enumerate adds a counter to an iterable.
pairs.feedback_wrong: Incorrect. That describes .items() or np.nditer().

pairs.match: Iterates over dictionary key-value pairs
wrong_matches: Iterates over list with index and value
Iterates over array elements in memory order
pairs.feedback_correct: Correct! .items() yields (key, value) tuples.
pairs.feedback_wrong: Incorrect. That describes enumerate or np.nditer.

pairs.match: Returns 3x2 array of uniform random numbers between 0 and 1
wrong_matches: Returns random integers from low to high
Sets seed for reproducible randomness
pairs.feedback_correct: Correct! rand generates floats in [0,1).
pairs.feedback_wrong: Incorrect. That would be randint or seed.

pairs.match: Initializes generator for reproducible random sequences
wrong_matches: Generates random uniform floats
Generates random integers in a range
pairs.feedback_correct: Correct! Setting the same seed produces the same random numbers.
pairs.feedback_wrong: Incorrect. That describes rand or randint.

pairs.match: Returns 0 or 1 randomly (coin toss) because high is exclusive
wrong_matches: Returns float between 0 and 1
Returns integer between 1 and 2 inclusive
pairs.feedback_correct: Correct! randint(low, high) gives [low, high).
pairs.feedback_wrong: Incorrect. That describes rand or mis-specified randint.
